subject: Product Management
title: Simpson's Paradox
description: A quick win: understand Simpson's Paradox.

hook.text: A variant wins overall but loses in every individual segment. How? Simpson's Paradox.

buildup.text: Imagine: variant B converts better with mobile users AND desktop users. But overall, variant A wins. The paradox is real.

discovery.text: It happens when group sizes are unbalanced.

twist.text: Simpson's Paradox means aggregate data can tell the opposite story from segmented data.

climax.text: The defense: always segment your experiment results. Check key dimensions: device, country, plan type, and new vs returning users.

punchline.text: The average can lie. Always look beneath it. Segment everything.

quiz.question: What is Simpson's Paradox?
options: When aggregate data shows the opposite result from segmented data due to unbalanced group sizes
When experiments always produce paradoxical results
A statistical test for sample size
